We recommended and the Management, through the Municipal Accountant, agreed to:
Collaborate with the Project Monitoring Committee, Municipal Engineering’s Office and BAC Secretariats to develop a strategy for the timely submission of completion reports and other relevant documents to ensure timely recording of completed projects, particularly at year-end; and
Recognize both the asset and liability accounts for the completed projects, along with their corresponding monthly depreciation following the PAJE below:

We recommended and the Management, through the Municipal Accountant, agreed to re-classify the birthing center and infirmary, along with their corresponding accumulated depreciation to their appropriate PPE accounts following the proposed PAJE below:

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Treasurer, who also act as the GSO, Municipal Accountant and other concerned personnel agreed to:
Collaborate and develop a systematic approach to meet the accounting and reporting requirements stipulated in COA Circular No. 2015-008; and
Attend or conduct training sessions or workshops, in coordination with DILG Regional Office VII, to stay informed on updates and changes in LRAMS and to facilitate the road network reports that is compliant with LRAMS guidelines.

We recommended and the Management agreed to implement the following measures:
The Municipal Accountant recognize the income when the taxable event occurs and deduct advance payments received only from the initially set-up receivables corresponding to the applicable year for which they were paid;
The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer monitor the advance payments by providing a separate column in the schedule to properly allocate the amounts received to the applicable year for which they were intended; and
The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer conduct regular reconciliation of RPT and SET receivable accounts based on the RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card balances and implement necessary adjustments to ensure accuracy and validity of recorded receivables.

We recommended and the Management agreed, through the Inventory Committee, to complete the conduct of physical count of all LGU’s properties and undertake the one-time cleansing of PPE, adhering to the stated guidelines and procedures of COA Circular 2020-006, such as the preparation of PIP, to ensure that reliable PPE balances are established, verifiable as to their existence, conditions, and accountability.
We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to expedite the plans of establishing the GSO to relieve the MTO of the responsibility for supply and property management and to better manage the increasing number of PPEs in the Municipality.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer agreed to intensify their document tracing efforts and coordinate with other LGUs in Cebu to inquire the existence of outstanding receivables from the Municipality of Asturias that needs to be liquidated.
We also recommended which the Management have agreed to follow up on the status of the resolutions from Barangays Kaluangan, Sta. Rita, and Tubigagmanok to facilitate the return of their counterpart share totaling ₱450,000.00.

We recommended and the BAC Chairperson, in coordination with the OIC-Municipal Engineer (end-user), agreed to submit a written justification/explanation on the noted deficiency.
We further recommended and the BAC agreed to revisit the RIRR of RA No. 9184 and other relevant GPPB issuances and observe the prohibition against making reference to specific brand names.

We recommended and the Management, through the BAC, agreed to comply with the following:
Submit the APP, PMR and APCPI to the GPPB, together with the PDF files and the certification, duly signed by the Head of the BAC Secretariat on the required posting requirements of APP and PMR, as provided in GPPB Circular No. 02-2020;
Prepare the PMR and APCPI results using the form or tool prescribed by the GPPB; and
Attend or conduct training sessions or workshops, in coordination with GPPB recognized trainers, to stay updated on changes and updates in procurement regulations and to assist in preparing the necessary procurement reports in accordance with GPPB guidelines.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to issue a certification stating that previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for as an attachment to the next cash advance disbursement voucher of the same accountable officer.
We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to specify the exact date of recording in the JEV to establish the official date of liquidation.
Lastly, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, agreed to increase the DO’s amount of bond.

We recommended and the Management, through the Municipal Treasurer, agreed to review and ensure that all lease contracts executed with the market stallholders include all relevant and required information to facilitate efficient and effective enforcement of the duties and obligations, while safeguarding the interests of all parties involved.

We recommended and the Management agreed, through coordination among the BAC, MEO and the MPDC, to schedule the procurement activities as early as the first or second quarter of the year to facilitate the implementation of planned development projects within the target year.
We likewise recommended that Management, through the coordination between the MEO and the BAC, to consider adopting the EPA in accordance with Appendix 31 of the RIRR of RA 9184 to ensure the timely implementation of development projects within the budget year.

We recommended and the MBO and LDRRM Officer agreed to comply with the requirement to appropriate in its annual budget at least five per cent (5%) of its estimated revenue from regular sources for LDRRMF pursuant to the requirements of RA 10121 and JMC 2013-1.
We also recommended which the MBO and LDRRM Office likewise agreed to appropriate the under-allocation of ₱102,400.00 to additional disaster risk-related PPAs in the LDRRM budget.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to expedite the review of dormant beginning and negative balances to determine the necessary adjusting entries to resolve or correct these balances.
We also recommended and the AAO for GSIS agreed to ensure timely updating of member records and rates to avoid discrepancies between the amounts billed by GSIS and the actual amounts withheld from employees.

The Municipal Mayor institutionalize the establishment of a functional Internal Audit Service/Unit as an integral part of the agency pursuant to AO No. 70 and adopt the Internal Audit Manual for Local Government Units, which was circularized under Local Budget Circular No. 110 dated June 10, 2016, as guide for its operation.

Install a sound system of internal control to strengthen accountability, ensure ethical, economical, efficient and effective operations, to improve the quality and quantity of outputs and to better respond to the needs of the constituents.
Submit an explanation/justification on the observed excess of the project cost of ₱197,595.96, for evaluation by the Audit Team,and henceforth, be more careful in the preparation of cost estimates.

Require the Engineering and other concerned offices to prepare a catch-up plan to expedite/fast-track the completion of all delayed/unimplemented projects and impose applicable sanctions including liquidated damages on contractors based on the applicable rate on the cost of unperformed portion of the contract for everyday of delay.

Ensure that detailed engineering investigations, surveys and designs, including right of way and other permits for the project would be conscientiously undertaken.

Issue a certification to verify that detailed engineering has been completed before proceeding to the bidding and award of contract to prevent incurrence of delays in the project completion and contract implementation.
Abide with the limitations set by the Procurement Law and its RIRR as well as the Manual of Procedures for the Procurement of Infrastructure Projects for local government units.

The Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Budget Officer in coordination with the Municipal Engineer to review the unexpended balances and unobligated appropriation of projects carried over as CY 2022 Continuing Appropriations to determine which are eligible as continuing PPAs pursuant to the provision of Sections 321 and 322 of RA No. 7160 and thereafter, consider initiating the process of reversion of these unobligated appropriation which can no longer be pursued by the Municipality to be used for other developmental projects.

Provide the Audit Team with a copy of the approved CY 2022 APP including any supplemental thereto prepared in accordance with RA 9184 as well as PPMPs or an explanation why the same could not be submitted; and

Ensure that the preparation of APP is consistent with the annual budget and in accordance with RA No. 9184 and its related issuances.

Establish clear cut guidelines on the grant of AICS, such as the qualification of indigents, amount and frequency of assistance, documentary requirements, and the like, concurred by the Sangguniang Bayan, to provide proper control in the disbursement of public funds.

The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer coordinate with the implementing department and expedite implementation of the unimplemented PPAS as outlined in the LDRRMFIP to achieve maximum benefits for the constituents of the Municipality and ultimately, serve its purpose of ensuring resiliency of the community during calamities. 

In the event that said PPAs shall be determined to be not viable, the Management shall evaluate the same and recommend for reprogramming and prepare and submit an amended LDRRMFIP duly approved by the Local Chief Executive and Sangguniang Bayan.

Facilitate the amendment of the provision in the GAD Code and ensure that the cost of implementation of GAD PPAs shall not be less than five percent of the total Annual Budget,

Include in the GPB appropriations the GAD-related trainings in gender analysis specifically on the administration of HGDG to be able to appropriately attribute a portion or the whole budget of the LGU’s PPAs to the GAD budget supporting gender-responsive PPAs; and  

Seek assistance from oversight agencies such as DILG, PCW, DBM and NEDA in pursuing gender mainstreaming and gradually increase the gender-responsiveness of the LGU PPAs.

The BAC should always be updated with the issuances and advisories of the GPPB by visiting their website regularly to avoid   non-compliance in any issuance related to procurement activities of the agency.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant initiate the write-off of the subject dormant accounts by observing the guidelines outlined in COA Circular 2016-005.  It is also enjoined that, henceforth, Management conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts to ensure that these are collected when these become due and demandable and that cash advances and fund transfers are liquidated within the prescribed period depending upon their nature and purpose pursuant to Item 6 of the same circular.

We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant agreed to use the account Advances to Contractors in recording the payment for mobilization in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that Management direct the Accounting unit to identify the details of those PPE items purchased prior to the implementation of NGAS and provide for depreciation and impairment following the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2004-003 and IPSAS 21. For those items that are already non-existing/ dilapidated/ abandoned we recommend that these will be included in the one-time cleansing of the Municipality’s PPE accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.
It is also recommended that the Local Chief Executive institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties upon the completion of the physical count of the Inventory Committee pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD 1445 and Section 379-381 of RA 7160 and in accordance with the procedure cited in COA Circular No. 89-296. The Management is enjoined as well to consider establishing the General Services Office (GSO) to facilitate effective management and control over the Municipality’s properties.

We recommended that Management conduct regular monitoring and analysis to ensure that fund transfers are liquidated within the prescribed period or upon completion of the projects. Also, require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the return of the excess funds received and those that are no longer needed to the source agencies or transfer as income of the general fund subject to the consent/approval of the grantor.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed that upon receipt of the certified list of uncollected market rentals from the Treasurer’s office, she would  prepare the necessary journal entry to correct the understatement of this year’s income and receivable account. Henceforth, at  year-end, the Municipal Treasurer shall submit to the Municipal Accountant the aforesaid certified list so that appropriate recording of the same can be made in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to include in the program of work the cost of billboards for the publicity of all its programs/projects/activities including those implemented By Administration in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 to inform the public on the essential features/elements of all the projects implemented by the Municipality to promote transparency and accountability.
We recommended that Management submit the contracts with complete supporting documents within the time frame required by COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate a timely technical review.

We recommended that henceforth, the BAC comply with the pertinent provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 before a recommendation for the award of contract to the HoPE shall be made to avoid exposing the Municipality to legal and financial risks.

CY 2019 was the first year of the Municipality of Asturias to have an LCPC Plan.  The plan was made on the last week of March and was submitted to the Sangguniang Bayan for the approval of funds. observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC Secretariat be required to post the result of the public bidding conducted in the PhilGEPS, website of the agency and in the bulletin board/s provided by the LGU for various postings/ announcements so that the public may know what the municipal government is doing pursuant to Section 37 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Results of the bidding are posted in the bulletin board provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned office and the created inventory committee if there’s any, shall conduct immediately the actual physical inventory taking of all the properties of the municipality. Reconcile the results with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: To conduct physical count of all property, plant and equipment.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer’s Office had submitted the RPCPPE but this remained not reconciled with the Accounting records.  This observation is reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should strictly enforce the provisions stipulated in the GAA pertaining to net take home pay to promote an efficient and effective public service by ensuring that government personnel have enough to support for their daily needs until the next payday.
observations.reason: The municipal employees have some financial constraints that need to be addressed to immediately.

observations.recommendation: Management should secure land titles on the purchase of lots, otherwise the amount paid shall be disallowed in audit and the officials who approved the said purchase shall be held liable.
observations.managementAction: The special patent applications is in process in accordance with DENR Administrative Order (DAO) No. 2016-21 dated June 30, 2016, entitled “Guidelines Governing the Processing of Request for Proclamations and Issuance of Special Patents over Agricultural Lands” as per DENR-RO VII Memo dated No. 10, 2016.  Attached is Annex A to D for reference.
observations.reason:
The LGU is waiting for the approved survey plan.

Progress report from the LGU as of November 2016, the application for special free patent of the land is already at the office CENR Officer for appropriate action in that office.

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned personnel to refund immediately the CNA Incentive received, otherwise, all those who authorized the payment of the subject incentive shall be held personally liable for the amount involved.  Henceforth, strict compliance with the provisions of DBM Circular No. 2006-01 is enjoined to avoid personal liability.
observations.managementAction: Partially collected from those who resigned.

observations.recommendation:
The Municipality should strictly enforce the immediate deposit of collections and that there should be no cash in vault higher than the bonded  amount  of   the  treasurer  to avoid another loss of government funds considering the June 2010 shortage of P1,305,358.99 due to lapping.

The Municipal Treasurer should restitute the amount of P91,000.20, representing the remaining unrecovered amount. 

The agency should strictly and consistently observe internal controls set in safeguarding government funds and the reportorial requirements so as to avoid loss of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Collections are deposited to depository bank thrice a week.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Accounting and Property Section should reconcile their records on PPE and investigate the causes of the discrepancies so that these can be adjusted accordingly. Henceforth, this activity should be performed on a regular basis.

We further recommend that a Committee on Inventory be created to conduct a complete inventory particularly on Land, Land Improvements and Buildings accounts to ascertain the existence and reliability of the account balances presented in the financial statements.

Unserviceable Property, Plant and Equipment had increased from P13,431,376.22 in CY 2011 to P15,418,992.55 in CY 2012 and were not immediately disposed of, contrary to the provision of Section 79 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 83-206.   Likewise, out of P15,418,992.55 cost of Unserviceable Properties, only 37% or P8,457,850.00 were requested for inspection and appraisal by COA for disposal, pursuant to Section 79 of PD 445.  Delay on the disposal of these properties had exposed them to fast deterioration thus prevented the municipality from obtaining higher proceeds from sale thereof.

Recommendation:

It is recommended that the Committee on Disposal should conduct an inspection on the condition of unserviceable properties and equipment, make an appraisal of the value of all disposable properties and submit the I & I Report to COA for inspection and appraisal and determine the mode of disposal to be undertaken, to facilitate the immediate disposal to prevent further deterioration and decrease in salvage values.
observations.managementAction: The unreconciled figure is being looked into regularly to arrive at the correct figure.
observations.reason: The recorded PPE remained not reconciled with the RPCPPE as of December 31, 2016, hence reiterated in the report.
